import json
import uuid

from sqlalchemy import ForeignKey, Integer, JSON, String
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship

from app.db.session import Base
from app.models.mixins import TimestampMixin, UUIDPrimaryKeyMixin
from app.security.encryption import PgcryptoEncryptedText


class ParticipantProfile(UUIDPrimaryKeyMixin, TimestampMixin, Base):
    __tablename__ = "participant_profiles"

    user_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("users.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), unique=True, nullable=False)
    profile_status: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False, default="in_progress")
    demographics_json: Mapped[dict] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False, default=dict)
    medical_flags_json: Mapped[dict] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False, default=dict)
    activity_json: Mapped[dict] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False, default=dict)

import json

from app.models.notification import Notification
from app.models.plan import NutritionPlan, NutritionPlanVersion, PlanPhase
from app.models.profile import ParticipantProfile, ParticipantProfileVersion
from app.models.settings import SystemSetting
from app.models.user import NotificationPreference, User
from app.security.passwords import hash_password
from app.utils.datetime import add_hours, utc_now


def _ensure_audit_immutability_guards(db) -> None:
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_audit_log_delete()
            RETURNS trigger
            LANGUAGE plpgsql
            AS $$
            BEGIN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'audit_logs is append-only and cannot be deleted';
            END;
            $$;
            """
        )
    )
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION restrict_audit_log_update()

    reviewer = User(username="reviewer_demo", full_name="Riley Reviewer", email_optional="reviewer@example.local", role=Role.REVIEWER, status=UserStatus.ACTIVE, password_hash=reviewer_password, is_active=True)
    admin = User(username="admin_demo", full_name="Avery Admin", email_optional="admin@example.local", role=Role.ADMINISTRATOR, status=UserStatus.ACTIVE, password_hash=admin_password, is_active=True)
    db.add_all([participant, reviewer, admin])

    for user, password_hash in [(participant, participant_password), (reviewer, reviewer_password), (admin, admin_password)]:

    profile = ParticipantProfile(
        user_id=participant.id,

        demographics_json={"age": 34, "gender": "female"},
        medical_flags_json={"has_allergies": True},
        activity_json={"activity_level": "moderate"},
        anthropometrics_json={"height_cm": 170, "weight_kg": 68},

    )
    db.add(profile)
    db.flush()
    profile_version = ParticipantProfileVersion(profile_id=profile.id, version_number=1, snapshot_json={"profile_status": "complete"}, change_summary_json=[], created_by=participant.username)
    db.add(profile_version)
    db.flush()
    profile.current_version_id = profile_version.id

    plan = NutritionPlan(participant_id=participant.id, profile_id=profile.id, title="12 Week Energy Balance", duration_weeks=12, goal_category="weight_management", status="draft")

    db.add(plan_version)
    db.flush()
    plan.current_version_id = plan_version.id
    for phase_number in range(1, 4):
        db.add(PlanPhase(plan_version_id=plan_version.id, phase_number=phase_number, week_start=(phase_number - 1) * 4 + 1, week_end=phase_number * 4, objective=f"Phase {phase_number} objective", calorie_target=2000 - phase_number * 50, macro_targets_json={"protein": 120}, habits_json=["hydrate"], success_metrics_json=["weekly consistency"]))

    package = DeclarationPackage(

        current_plan_version_id=plan_version.id,
        state=DeclarationState.SUBMITTED,
        submitted_at=utc_now(),
        review_due_at=add_hours(72),
    )
    db.add(package)
    db.flush()
    assignment = ReviewAssignment(
        package_id=package.id,
        reviewer_id=reviewer.id,
        assigned_at=utc_now(),
        review_due_at=package.review_due_at,
        priority="normal",
        status=ReviewAssignmentStatus.QUEUED,
    )
    db.add(assignment)
    db.flush()
    package.current_review_assignment_id = assignment.id
    db.add(DeclarationStateHistory(package_id=package.id, from_state=None, to_state=DeclarationState.SUBMITTED, reason_code=None, reason_text=None, changed_by=participant.username, changed_at=utc_now()))
    db.add(PackageVersion(package_id=package.id, version_number=1, state=DeclarationState.SUBMITTED, profile_version_id=profile_version.id, plan_version_id=plan_version.id, snapshot_json={"state": "submitted"}, change_summary_json=[], created_by=participant.username))

    db.add(Notification(user_id=participant.id, type=NotificationType.MANDATORY_COMPLIANCE_ALERT, severity=NotificationSeverity.CRITICAL, title="Compliance review pending", message="Your declaration package requires review completion.", link_path=f"/app/participant/declarations/{package.id}", is_muted_snapshot=False))

        "audit_retention_years": 7,
    }.items():
        db.add(SystemSetting(key=key, value_json={"value": value}, updated_by="system", updated_at=utc_now()))
    db.commit()

import json

from sqlalchemy import text

from app.core.constants import DeclarationState, NotificationSeverity, NotificationType, ReviewAssignmentStatus, Role, UserStatus
from app.models.auth import PasswordHistory
from app.models.declaration import DeclarationPackage, DeclarationStateHistory, PackageVersion, ReviewAssignment
from app.models.notification import Notification
from app.models.plan import NutritionPlan, NutritionPlanVersion, PlanPhase
from app.models.profile import ParticipantProfile, ParticipantProfileVersion
from app.models.settings import SystemSetting
from app.models.user import NotificationPreference, User
from app.security.encryption import EncryptionService
from app.security.passwords import hash_password
from app.utils.datetime import add_hours, utc_now


def _ensure_audit_immutability_guards(db) -> None:
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_audit_log_delete()
            RETURNS trigger
            LANGUAGE plpgsql
            AS $$
            BEGIN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'audit_logs is append-only and cannot be deleted';
            END;
            $$;
            """
        )
    )
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION restrict_audit_log_update()
            RETURNS trigger
            LANGUAGE plpgsql
            AS $$
            BEGIN
                IF (to_jsonb(NEW) - 'archived_at') IS DISTINCT FROM (to_jsonb(OLD) - 'archived_at') THEN
                    RAISE EXCEPTION 'audit_logs is immutable; only archived_at may be updated';
                END IF;
                RETURN NEW;
            END;
            $$;
            """
        )
    )
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            DO $$
            BEGIN
                IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname = 'trg_prevent_audit_log_delete') THEN
                    CREATE TRIGGER trg_prevent_audit_log_delete
                    BEFORE DELETE ON audit_logs
                    FOR EACH ROW
                    EXECUTE FUNCTION prevent_audit_log_delete();
                END IF;
            END;
            $$;
            """
        )
    )
    db.execute(
        text(
            """
            DO $$
            BEGIN
                IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname = 'trg_restrict_audit_log_update') THEN
                    CREATE TRIGGER trg_restrict_audit_log_update
                    BEFORE UPDATE ON audit_logs
                    FOR EACH ROW
                    EXECUTE FUNCTION restrict_audit_log_update();
                END IF;
            END;
            $$;
            """
        )
    )


def seed_demo_data(db):
    if db.query(User).count() > 0:
        return
    EncryptionService().ensure_pgcrypto_extension(db)
    _ensure_audit_immutability_guards(db)

    participant_password = hash_password("Participant#2026")
    reviewer_password = hash_password("Reviewer#2026")
    admin_password = hash_password("Admin#2026Secure")

from sqlalchemy import text

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_profile_sensitive_payload_uses_db_side_encryption(client, db_session):
    headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    payload = {
        "profile_status": "complete",
        "demographics_json": {"age": 33},
        "medical_flags_json": {"diabetes": False},
        "activity_json": {"activity_level": "moderate"},
        "anthropometrics_json": {"height_cm": 170},
        "sensitive": {
            "allergy_detail": "Sesame",
            "clinician_notes": "Avoid sesame during elimination phase.",
        },
    }

    saved = client.put("/api/v1/profiles/me", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert saved.status_code == 200
    saved_profile = saved.json()["data"]
    assert saved_profile["sensitive"] == payload["sensitive"]

    fetched = client.get("/api/v1/profiles/me", headers=headers)
    assert fetched.status_code == 200
    assert fetched.json()["data"]["sensitive"] == payload["sensitive"]

    stored_payload = db_session.execute(
        text("SELECT encrypted_payload FROM participant_profiles WHERE id = :id"),
        {"id": saved_profile["id"]},
    ).scalar_one()
    assert isinstance(stored_payload, str)
    assert stored_payload.startswith("-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----")
    assert payload["sensitive"]["allergy_detail"] not in stored_payload
    assert payload["sensitive"]["clinician_notes"] not in stored_payload

from sqlalchemy import text

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_profile_sensitive_payload_uses_db_side_encryption(client, db_session):
    headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    payload = {
        "profile_status": "complete",
        "demographics_json": {"age": 33},
        "medical_flags_json": {"diabetes": False},
        "activity_json": {"activity_level": "moderate"},
        "anthropometrics_json": {"height_cm": 170},
        "sensitive": {
            "allergy_detail": "Sesame",
            "clinician_notes": "Avoid sesame during elimination phase.",
        },
    }

    saved = client.put("/api/v1/profiles/me", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert saved.status_code == 200
    saved_profile = saved.json()["data"]
    for key, value in payload["sensitive"].items():
        assert saved_profile["sensitive"][key] == value

    fetched = client.get("/api/v1/profiles/me", headers=headers)
    assert fetched.status_code == 200
    for key, value in payload["sensitive"].items():
        assert fetched.json()["data"]["sensitive"][key] == value

    stored_payload = db_session.execute(
        text("SELECT encrypted_payload FROM participant_profiles WHERE id = :id"),
        {"id": saved_profile["id"]},
    ).scalar_one()
    assert isinstance(stored_payload, str)
    assert stored_payload.startswith("-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----")
    assert payload["sensitive"]["allergy_detail"] not in stored_payload
    assert payload["sensitive"]["clinician_notes"] not in stored_payload

**Finalizing project details**

I need to compile a final concise summary that includes files and tests. I’ll mention that running the Alembic upgrade head went smoothly. It’s important to note the seed safeguard during the test reset too. I should add the profile API serialization since it might be necessary to expose sensitive decrypted fields. Also, I’ll include file references for clarity. Let's make sure everything is organized neatly for easy access!

Switched profile storage to pgcrypto-only encryption and removed the app-side AES layer from the `encrypted_payload` flow.

- `backend/app/services/profile_service.py:1` now stores `sensitive` as `json.dumps(...)` and reads it back with `json.loads(...)`, so the `PgcryptoEncryptedText` column handles encryption/decryption by itself.
- `backend/app/api/v1/profiles.py:7` now serializes profile responses through `ProfileRead`, and `backend/app/models/profile.py:32` adds a `sensitive` property so decrypted sensitive fields are exposed cleanly while `encrypted_payload` stays internal.
- `backend/app/main.py:18` ensures `pgcrypto` exists at startup, and `backend/app/db/seed.py:1` now seeds plaintext JSON into the encrypted column while still re-ensuring the extension after schema resets in tests.
- Added a one-time Alembic data migration in `backend/migrations/versions/6f1a2c9d4e5b_migrate_profile_payloads_to_pgcrypto_only.py:1` that detects legacy double-encrypted rows, decrypts the inner AES payload, and rewrites them as pgcrypto-only values.
- Added DB-side encryption verification in `API_tests/test_profile_encryption_api.py:1`, asserting API reads return decrypted sensitive fields while raw SQL sees armored PGP ciphertext.

Verified in Docker:

## Prompt 3: Verify and Harden Audit Actor Attribution for Admin Password Reset

### Role

You are a Senior Full-Stack Engineer working on the NutriDeclare Offline Compliance System. Your goal is to verify that the audit trail for admin-initiated password resets correctly attributes the action to the **admin** user (the actor), not the **target** user whose password is being reset.

### Root Cause Analysis

The architecture review flagged this at **Issue #3 (Medium priority)**: "Audit actor attribution for admin reset uses target user context" with evidence at `backend/app/api/v1/admin.py:35` and `backend/app/services/auth_service.py:131`.

Upon code inspection, the current implementation appears to have **already addressed this concern** in some paths, but needs verification and potential hardening:

1. **`backend/app/api/v1/admin.py`, lines 33-37** — The `reset_password` endpoint:
   ```python
   def reset_password(user_id: str, payload: AdminPasswordResetRequest, db: DBSession, user=Depends(require_roles(Role.ADMINISTRATOR))):
       target = UserService(db).get_user(user_id)
       AuthService(db).admin_reset_password(target, payload.new_password, admin_user=user)
   ```
   Here `user` is the authenticated admin (from `require_roles(Role.ADMINISTRATOR)`), and `target` is the user whose password is being reset. The call passes `admin_user=user` — this is **correct**.

2. **`backend/app/services/auth_service.py`, lines 128-151** — The `admin_reset_password()` method:
   ```python
   def admin_reset_password(self, user: User, new_password: str, *, admin_user: User) -> None:
       self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=True, actor_user=admin_user)
   ```
   This passes `actor_user=admin_user` to `_set_new_password`. Inside `_set_new_password` (line 131-160):
   ```python
   actor_id = actor_user.id if actor_user else user.id
   self.audit.log(
       actor_user_id=actor_id,
       action_type="password_change",
       entity_type="user",
       entity_id=str(user.id),
       metadata={
           "forced": force_password_change,
           "target_user_id": str(user.id),
           "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
       },
   )
   ```
   The `actor_user_id` is set to `actor_id` which is `admin_user.id` — this is **correct**.

3. **However**, the audit `metadata` (lines 146-150) includes `"target_user_id": str(user.id)` and `"admin_initiated": True` — but it does NOT include the admin user's ID or username in the metadata. This means while the `actor_user_id` column is correct, the metadata lacks the admin's identity for forensic clarity.

4. **Additionally**, verify there are no other code paths where `_set_new_password` is called without `actor_user`:
   - `change_password()` (line 120-123): Calls `self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=False)` — no `actor_user`, so `actor_id` defaults to `user.id`. This is correct because the user is changing their own password.
   - `complete_forced_password_change()` (line 125-126): Same pattern, user acting on themselves. Correct.

### What You Must Do

#### Step 1: Enrich audit metadata with admin identity

- **File**: `backend/app/services/auth_service.py`
- **Lines 141-151**: Modify the `self.audit.log()` call inside `_set_new_password` to include the admin user's ID and username in the metadata when an admin initiates the action:

```python
# BEFORE (lines 141-151):
actor_id = actor_user.id if actor_user else user.id
self.audit.log(
    actor_user_id=actor_id,
    action_type="password_change",
    entity_type="user",
    entity_id=str(user.id),
    metadata={
        "forced": force_password_change,
        "target_user_id": str(user.id),
        "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
    },
)

# AFTER:
actor_id = actor_user.id if actor_user else user.id
metadata = {
    "forced": force_password_change,
    "target_user_id": str(user.id),
    "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
}
if actor_user is not None:
    metadata["admin_user_id"] = str(actor_user.id)
    metadata["admin_username"] = actor_user.username
self.audit.log(
    actor_user_id=actor_id,
    action_type="password_change",
    entity_type="user",
    entity_id=str(user.id),
    metadata=metadata,
)
```

#### Step 2: Distinguish the `action_type` for admin-initiated resets

Currently, both user-initiated password changes and admin-initiated resets use `action_type="password_change"`. For audit forensic clarity, distinguish them:

- **File**: `backend/app/services/auth_service.py`
- **Line 142** (the `action_type` parameter in the `self.audit.log` call):
  ```python
  # Change:
  action_type="password_change",
  # To:
  action_type="admin_password_reset" if actor_user is not None else "password_change",
  ```

#### Step 3: Add a test to verify audit attribution

- **File**: Create or update `API_tests/test_admin_api.py` or `unit_tests/test_audit_attribution.py`
- Add a test that:
  1. Logs in as an admin user
  2. Calls `POST /api/v1/admin/users/{target_user_id}/reset-password` with a new password
  3. Queries the audit log (via `GET /api/v1/audit/logs` or by database inspection)
  4. **Asserts** the most recent audit entry has:
     - `actor_user_id` equal to the **admin user's ID** (NOT the target user's ID)
     - `action_type` equal to `"admin_password_reset"`
     - `entity_id` equal to the **target user's ID**
     - `metadata_json.admin_initiated` equals `True`
     - `metadata_json.admin_user_id` equals the admin's user ID string
     - `metadata_json.target_user_id` equals the target user's ID string

#### Step 4: Verify the admin's `create_user` and `update_user` audit trails

While you're at it, verify that `UserService.create_user()` and `UserService.update_user()` (called from `backend/app/api/v1/admin.py`, lines 25 and 30) also correctly use the admin's identity as `actor_user_id` in their audit log calls:

- **File**: `backend/app/services/user_service.py`
- Check that `create_user(user, payload)` and `update_user(user, user_id, payload)` use the `user` parameter (the admin) as the actor in `self.audit.log(actor_user_id=user.id, ...)`, NOT the new/target user's ID.

### Expected Result After Fix

- Admin-initiated password resets produce audit log entries where:
  - `actor_user_id` = the admin who performed the action
  - `action_type` = `"admin_password_reset"` (distinctly identifiable)
  - `entity_id` = the target user whose password was changed
  - `metadata_json` includes both `admin_user_id`, `admin_username`, and `target_user_id`
- Self-initiated password changes still use `action_type="password_change"` with the user as both actor and target
- Review criteria **Issue #3** (audit actor attribution) is resolved
- The audit trail is forensically complete and unambiguous
- Review criteria **§2.1.8** (immutable audit) remains **Partially Pass** or improves (the audit attribution sub-issue is resolved)

---

**Inspecting files for plan**

I need to make sure I'm sticking to the read-only mode, which means I can only use tools like read, grep, glob, or bash for that purpose. I should outline a plan and possibly include one question if it helps clarify things. So, let's take a closer look at the relevant files to see what I might find there. I want to ensure I'm following the guidelines strictly while doing this.

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.api.deps import DBSession, require_roles
from app.core.constants import Role
from app.core.responses import success_response
from app.schemas.admin import SettingsUpdateRequest
from app.schemas.users import AdminPasswordResetRequest, AdminUserCreate, AdminUserUpdate
from app.services.admin_service import AdminService
from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
from app.services.user_service import UserService
from app.utils.pagination import PaginationParams, paginate_query

router = APIRouter()


@router.get("/users")
def list_users(db: DBSession, params: PaginationParams = Depends(), user=Depends(require_roles(Role.ADMINISTRATOR))):
    service = AdminService(db)
    paginated = paginate_query(db, service.users_stmt(), params)
    return success_response(paginated.items, meta=paginated.meta)

from app.core.constants import UserStatus
from app.core.exceptions import NotFoundError
from app.models.user import NotificationPreference, User
from app.repositories.user_repository import UserRepository
from app.security.passwords import hash_password, validate_password_policy
from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
from app.utils.datetime import utc_now


class UserService:
    def __init__(self, db):
        self.db = db
        self.repo = UserRepository(db)
        self.audit = AuditService(db)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.repo.get_by_id(user_id)
        if not user:
            raise NotFoundError("User not found.")
        return user

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_admin_disable_and_reset(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(user for user in users if user["username"] == "participant_demo")
    disabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "disabled", "is_active": False}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert disabled.status_code == 200
    denied_login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "Participant#2026"})
    assert denied_login.status_code == 403
    enabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "active", "is_active": True}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert enabled.status_code == 200
    reset = client.post(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}/reset-password", json={"new_password": "ResetPass#2026"}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert reset.status_code == 200
    login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "ResetPass#2026"})
    assert login.status_code == 200
    assert login.json()["data"]["force_password_change"] is True

I checked the implementation, and the core actor attribution is already correct.

- `backend/app/api/v1/admin.py:33` passes the authenticated admin as `admin_user=user` into `AuthService.admin_reset_password(...)`.
- `backend/app/services/auth_service.py:128` forwards that admin into `_set_new_password(..., actor_user=admin_user)`.
- `backend/app/services/auth_service.py:140` sets `actor_id = actor_user.id if actor_user else user.id`, so admin resets already log the admin in `actor_user_id`.
- `backend/app/services/user_service.py:35` and `backend/app/services/user_service.py:54` already use the acting admin as `actor_user_id` for `admin_user_create` and `admin_user_update`.

What still needs hardening is exactly what you identified:

- `backend/app/services/auth_service.py:143` still uses `action_type="password_change"` for both self-change and admin reset.

files.before:
import logging

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.constants import Role, UserStatus
from app.core.exceptions import AuthenticationError, AuthorizationError, ConflictError, ValidationError
from app.models.auth import PasswordHistory, RefreshToken
from app.models.user import NotificationPreference, User
from app.repositories.user_repository import UserRepository
from app.security.captcha import verify_captcha
from app.security.passwords import ensure_not_in_history, hash_password, validate_password_policy, verify_password
from app.security.tokens import create_access_token, create_refresh_token, decode_refresh_token, hash_refresh_token
from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
from app.utils.datetime import add_minutes, utc_now


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class AuthService:
    def __init__(self, db: Session):
        self.db = db
        self.settings = get_settings()
        self.repo = UserRepository(db)
        self.audit = AuditService(db)

    def register_participant(self, username: str, full_name: str, password: str, email_optional: str | None = None) -> User:
        if self.repo.get_by_username(username):
            raise ConflictError("Username already exists.")
        validate_password_policy(password)
        password_hash = hash_password(password)
        user = User(
            username=username,
            full_name=full_name,
            email_optional=email_optional,
            role=Role.PARTICIPANT,
            status=UserStatus.ACTIVE,
            password_hash=password_hash,
            is_active=True,
        )
        self.db.add(user)
        self.db.flush()
        self.db.add(NotificationPreference(user_id=user.id))
        self.db.add(PasswordHistory(user_id=user.id, password_hash=password_hash, created_at=utc_now()))
        self.audit.log(actor_user_id=user.id, action_type="register", entity_type="user", entity_id=str(user.id), metadata={"username": username})
        logger.info("New participant registered", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "username": username})
        self.db.commit()
        self.db.refresh(user)
        return user

    def _check_lockout(self, user: User) -> None:
        if user.locked_until and user.locked_until > utc_now():
            raise AuthenticationError("Account is locked. Try again later.")

    def _register_failed_attempt(self, user: User) -> None:
        user.failed_login_attempts += 1
        logger.warning("Failed login attempt", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "attempts": user.failed_login_attempts})
        if user.failed_login_attempts >= self.settings.lockout_failed_attempts:
            user.locked_until = add_minutes(self.settings.lockout_minutes)
            logger.warning("Account locked", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "locked_until": user.locked_until.isoformat()})
        self.db.add(user)
        self.db.commit()

    def login(self, username: str, password: str, *, captcha_challenge_token: str | None = None, captcha_answer: str | None = None, user_agent: str | None = None) -> dict:
        user = self.repo.get_by_username(username)
        if not user:
            raise AuthenticationError("Invalid username or password.")
        self.ensure_user_active(user)
        self._check_lockout(user)
        if self.settings.enable_local_captcha and user.captcha_required:
            if not captcha_challenge_token or not captcha_answer:
                raise ValidationError("CAPTCHA is required.", field="captcha")
            verify_captcha(captcha_challenge_token, captcha_answer)
        if not verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
            self._register_failed_attempt(user)
            raise AuthenticationError("Invalid username or password.")
        user.failed_login_attempts = 0
        user.locked_until = None
        user.last_login_at = utc_now()
        access = create_access_token(str(user.id), {"role": user.role})
        refresh, refresh_hash, refresh_expires = create_refresh_token(str(user.id))
        self.db.add(RefreshToken(user_id=user.id, token_hash=refresh_hash, expires_at=refresh_expires, issued_at=utc_now(), user_agent=user_agent))
        self.db.add(user)
        self.audit.log(actor_user_id=user.id, action_type="login", entity_type="user", entity_id=str(user.id), metadata={})
        logger.info("User login successful", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "username": username})
        self.db.commit()
        return {
            "access_token": access,
            "refresh_token": refresh,
            "token_type": "bearer",
            "access_expires_in_minutes": self.settings.access_token_expire_minutes,
            "refresh_expires_in_days": self.settings.refresh_token_expire_days,
            "force_password_change": user.force_password_change,
        }

    def refresh(self, refresh_token: str) -> dict:
        payload = decode_refresh_token(refresh_token)
        token_row = self.repo.get_refresh_token(hash_refresh_token(refresh_token))
        if not token_row or token_row.revoked_at is not None or token_row.expires_at < utc_now():
            raise AuthenticationError("Refresh token is invalid or expired.")
        user = self.repo.get_by_id(token_row.user_id)
        if not user or not user.is_active or user.status != UserStatus.ACTIVE:
            raise AuthenticationError("Session expired or account disabled.")
        token_row.revoked_at = utc_now()
        access = create_access_token(str(user.id), {"role": user.role})
        new_refresh, new_hash, new_exp = create_refresh_token(str(user.id))
        self.db.add(RefreshToken(user_id=user.id, token_hash=new_hash, expires_at=new_exp, issued_at=utc_now(), rotated_from_id=token_row.id))
        self.db.add(token_row)
        self.db.commit()
        return {
            "access_token": access,
            "refresh_token": new_refresh,
            "token_type": "bearer",
            "access_expires_in_minutes": self.settings.access_token_expire_minutes,
            "refresh_expires_in_days": self.settings.refresh_token_expire_days,
            "force_password_change": user.force_password_change,
        }

    def change_password(self, user: User, current_password: str, new_password: str) -> None:
        if not verify_password(current_password, user.password_hash):
            raise AuthenticationError("Current password is incorrect.")
        self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=False)

    def complete_forced_password_change(self, user: User, new_password: str) -> None:
        self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=False)

    def admin_reset_password(self, user: User, new_password: str, *, admin_user: User) -> None:
        self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=True, actor_user=admin_user)

    def _set_new_password(self, user: User, new_password: str, *, force_password_change: bool, actor_user: User | None = None) -> None:
        validate_password_policy(new_password)
        hashes = [item.password_hash for item in self.repo.list_password_history(user.id)]
        ensure_not_in_history(new_password, hashes)
        new_hash = hash_password(new_password)
        user.password_hash = new_hash
        user.force_password_change = force_password_change
        self.db.add(user)
        self.db.add(PasswordHistory(user_id=user.id, password_hash=new_hash, created_at=utc_now()))
        actor_id = actor_user.id if actor_user else user.id
        self.audit.log(
            actor_user_id=actor_id,
            action_type="password_change",
            entity_type="user",
            entity_id=str(user.id),
            metadata={
                "forced": force_password_change,
                "target_user_id": str(user.id),
                "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
            },
        )
        logger.info(
            "Password changed",
            extra={
                "user_id": str(user.id),
                "forced": force_password_change,
                "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
            },
        )
        self.db.commit()

    @staticmethod
    def ensure_user_active(user: User) -> None:
        if not user or not user.is_active or user.status != UserStatus.ACTIVE:
            raise AuthorizationError("Account is disabled.")

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.constants import Role, UserStatus
from app.core.exceptions import AuthenticationError, AuthorizationError, ConflictError, ValidationError

from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
from app.utils.datetime import add_minutes, utc_now


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class AuthService:
    def __init__(self, db: Session):
        self.db = db
        self.settings = get_settings()
        self.repo = UserRepository(db)
        self.audit = AuditService(db)

    def register_participant(self, username: str, full_name: str, password: str, email_optional: str | None = None) -> User:
        if self.repo.get_by_username(username):
            raise ConflictError("Username already exists.")
        validate_password_policy(password)
        password_hash = hash_password(password)
        user = User(
            username=username,
            full_name=full_name,
            email_optional=email_optional,
            role=Role.PARTICIPANT,
            status=UserStatus.ACTIVE,
            password_hash=password_hash,
            is_active=True,
        )
        self.db.add(user)
        self.db.flush()
        self.db.add(NotificationPreference(user_id=user.id))
        self.db.add(PasswordHistory(user_id=user.id, password_hash=password_hash, created_at=utc_now()))
        self.audit.log(actor_user_id=user.id, action_type="register", entity_type="user", entity_id=str(user.id), metadata={"username": username})
        logger.info("New participant registered", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "username": username})
        self.db.commit()
        self.db.refresh(user)
        return user

    def _check_lockout(self, user: User) -> None:
        if user.locked_until and user.locked_until > utc_now():
            raise AuthenticationError("Account is locked. Try again later.")

    def _register_failed_attempt(self, user: User) -> None:
        user.failed_login_attempts += 1
        logger.warning("Failed login attempt", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "attempts": user.failed_login_attempts})
        if user.failed_login_attempts >= self.settings.lockout_failed_attempts:
            user.locked_until = add_minutes(self.settings.lockout_minutes)
            logger.warning("Account locked", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "locked_until": user.locked_until.isoformat()})
        self.db.add(user)
        self.db.commit()

    def login(self, username: str, password: str, *, captcha_challenge_token: str | None = None, captcha_answer: str | None = None, user_agent: str | None = None) -> dict:
        user = self.repo.get_by_username(username)
        if not user:
            raise AuthenticationError("Invalid username or password.")
        self.ensure_user_active(user)
        self._check_lockout(user)
        if self.settings.enable_local_captcha and user.captcha_required:
            if not captcha_challenge_token or not captcha_answer:
                raise ValidationError("CAPTCHA is required.", field="captcha")
            verify_captcha(captcha_challenge_token, captcha_answer)
        if not verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
            self._register_failed_attempt(user)
            raise AuthenticationError("Invalid username or password.")
        user.failed_login_attempts = 0
        user.locked_until = None
        user.last_login_at = utc_now()
        access = create_access_token(str(user.id), {"role": user.role})
        refresh, refresh_hash, refresh_expires = create_refresh_token(str(user.id))
        self.db.add(RefreshToken(user_id=user.id, token_hash=refresh_hash, expires_at=refresh_expires, issued_at=utc_now(), user_agent=user_agent))
        self.db.add(user)
        self.audit.log(actor_user_id=user.id, action_type="login", entity_type="user", entity_id=str(user.id), metadata={})
        logger.info("User login successful", extra={"user_id": str(user.id), "username": username})
        self.db.commit()
        return {
            "access_token": access,
            "refresh_token": refresh,
            "token_type": "bearer",
            "access_expires_in_minutes": self.settings.access_token_expire_minutes,
            "refresh_expires_in_days": self.settings.refresh_token_expire_days,
            "force_password_change": user.force_password_change,
        }

    def refresh(self, refresh_token: str) -> dict:
        payload = decode_refresh_token(refresh_token)
        token_row = self.repo.get_refresh_token(hash_refresh_token(refresh_token))
        if not token_row or token_row.revoked_at is not None or token_row.expires_at < utc_now():
            raise AuthenticationError("Refresh token is invalid or expired.")
        user = self.repo.get_by_id(token_row.user_id)
        if not user or not user.is_active or user.status != UserStatus.ACTIVE:
            raise AuthenticationError("Session expired or account disabled.")
        token_row.revoked_at = utc_now()
        access = create_access_token(str(user.id), {"role": user.role})
        new_refresh, new_hash, new_exp = create_refresh_token(str(user.id))
        self.db.add(RefreshToken(user_id=user.id, token_hash=new_hash, expires_at=new_exp, issued_at=utc_now(), rotated_from_id=token_row.id))
        self.db.add(token_row)
        self.db.commit()
        return {
            "access_token": access,
            "refresh_token": new_refresh,
            "token_type": "bearer",
            "access_expires_in_minutes": self.settings.access_token_expire_minutes,
            "refresh_expires_in_days": self.settings.refresh_token_expire_days,

        }

        if not verify_password(current_password, user.password_hash):

        self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=False)

    def admin_reset_password(self, user: User, new_password: str, *, admin_user: User) -> None:
        self._set_new_password(user, new_password, force_password_change=True, actor_user=admin_user)

    def _set_new_password(self, user: User, new_password: str, *, force_password_change: bool, actor_user: User | None = None) -> None:
        validate_password_policy(new_password)
        hashes = [item.password_hash for item in self.repo.list_password_history(user.id)]
        ensure_not_in_history(new_password, hashes)

        metadata = {
            "forced": force_password_change,
            "target_user_id": str(user.id),
            "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
        }
        if actor_user is not None:
            metadata["admin_user_id"] = str(actor_user.id)
            metadata["admin_username"] = actor_user.username
        self.audit.log(
            actor_user_id=actor_id,
            action_type="admin_password_reset" if actor_user is not None else "password_change",
            entity_type="user",
            entity_id=str(user.id),
            metadata=metadata,
        )
        logger.info(
            "Password changed",
            extra={
                "user_id": str(user.id),
                "forced": force_password_change,
                "admin_initiated": actor_user is not None,
            },
        )
        self.db.commit()

    @staticmethod
    def ensure_user_active(user: User) -> None:
        if not user or not user.is_active or user.status != UserStatus.ACTIVE:
            raise AuthorizationError("Account is disabled.")

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_admin_disable_and_reset(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(user for user in users if user["username"] == "participant_demo")
    disabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "disabled", "is_active": False}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert disabled.status_code == 200
    denied_login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "Participant#2026"})
    assert denied_login.status_code == 403
    enabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "active", "is_active": True}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert enabled.status_code == 200
    reset = client.post(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}/reset-password", json={"new_password": "ResetPass#2026"}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert reset.status_code == 200
    login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "ResetPass#2026"})
    assert login.status_code == 200
    assert login.json()["data"]["force_password_change"] is True


def test_admin_user_create_is_audit_logged_with_actor(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    created = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/users",
        json={"username": "audited_admin_create", "full_name": "Audited Create", "password": "AuditedCreate#2026", "role": "reviewer", "email_optional": "audited_create@example.local"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert created.status_code == 200

    me = client.get("/api/v1/auth/me", headers=admin_headers)
    admin_id = me.json()["data"]["id"]
    audit = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=1&page_size=200", headers=admin_headers)
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert any(item["action_type"] == "admin_user_create" and item["actor_user_id"] == admin_id for item in audit.json()["data"])


def test_admin_password_reset_audit_uses_admin_as_actor(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(user for user in users if user["username"] == "participant_demo")

    reset = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}/reset-password",
        json={"new_password": "ResetAudit#2026"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert reset.status_code == 200

    me = client.get("/api/v1/auth/me", headers=admin_headers)
    admin_id = me.json()["data"]["id"]

    audit = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=1&page_size=200", headers=admin_headers)
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    reset_events = [
        item
        for item in audit.json()["data"]
        if item["action_type"] == "password_change"
        and item["entity_type"] == "user"
        and item["entity_id"] == participant["id"]
        and item["metadata_json"].get("forced") is True
    ]
    assert reset_events
    event = reset_events[0]
    assert event["actor_user_id"] == admin_id
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("target_user_id") == participant["id"]
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("admin_initiated") is True

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_admin_disable_and_reset(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(user for user in users if user["username"] == "participant_demo")
    disabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "disabled", "is_active": False}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert disabled.status_code == 200
    denied_login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "Participant#2026"})
    assert denied_login.status_code == 403
    enabled = client.patch(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}", json={"status": "active", "is_active": True}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert enabled.status_code == 200
    reset = client.post(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}/reset-password", json={"new_password": "ResetPass#2026"}, headers=admin_headers)
    assert reset.status_code == 200
    login = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "participant_demo", "password": "ResetPass#2026"})
    assert login.status_code == 200
    assert login.json()["data"]["force_password_change"] is True


def test_admin_user_create_is_audit_logged_with_actor(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    created = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/users",
        json={"username": "audited_admin_create", "full_name": "Audited Create", "password": "AuditedCreate#2026", "role": "reviewer", "email_optional": "audited_create@example.local"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert created.status_code == 200

    me = client.get("/api/v1/auth/me", headers=admin_headers)
    admin_id = me.json()["data"]["id"]
    audit = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=1&page_size=200", headers=admin_headers)
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert any(item["action_type"] == "admin_user_create" and item["actor_user_id"] == admin_id for item in audit.json()["data"])


def test_admin_password_reset_audit_uses_admin_as_actor(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(user for user in users if user["username"] == "participant_demo")

    reset = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}/reset-password",
        json={"new_password": "ResetAudit#2026"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert reset.status_code == 200

    me = client.get("/api/v1/auth/me", headers=admin_headers)
    admin_id = me.json()["data"]["id"]
    admin_username = me.json()["data"]["username"]

    audit = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=1&page_size=200", headers=admin_headers)
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    reset_events = [
        item
        for item in audit.json()["data"]
        if item["action_type"] == "admin_password_reset"
        and item["entity_type"] == "user"
        and item["entity_id"] == participant["id"]
        and item["metadata_json"].get("forced") is True
    ]
    assert reset_events
    event = reset_events[0]
    assert event["actor_user_id"] == admin_id
    assert event["action_type"] == "admin_password_reset"
    assert event["entity_id"] == participant["id"]
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("target_user_id") == participant["id"]
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("admin_initiated") is True
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("admin_user_id") == admin_id
    assert event["metadata_json"].get("admin_username") == admin_username

content.text:
Hardened admin password reset audit attribution so the log is explicit about both the admin actor and the target account.

- `backend/app/services/auth_service.py:131` now builds audit metadata separately, adds `admin_user_id` and `admin_username` for admin-initiated resets, and uses `action_type="admin_password_reset"` instead of reusing `password_change`.
- Self-service password changes still keep `action_type="password_change"` and continue attributing the acting user to themselves.
- I verified `backend/app/services/user_service.py:35` and `backend/app/services/user_service.py:54` already log `admin_user_create` and `admin_user_update` with `actor_user_id=actor.id`, so no change was needed there.
- `API_tests/test_admin_api.py:37` now asserts the audit row uses the admin as `actor_user_id`, the target user as `entity_id`, and includes `admin_initiated`, `admin_user_id`, `admin_username`, and `target_user_id` in `metadata_json`.

Verified in Docker:
- `docker compose run --rm -v "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/nutrideclare-offline-compliance-system/repo:/workspace" -w /workspace backend pytest API_tests/test_admin_api.py API_tests/test_audit_api.py`
- Result: `5 passed`

If you want, I can run the broader API suite next to make sure the new `admin_password_reset` action type doesn’t affect any downstream audit consumers.
okay run the test

## Prompt 4 (Bonus — Medium Priority): Tighten Enum-Like Schema Fields from `str` to Proper Enums

### Role

You are a Senior Full-Stack Engineer working on the NutriDeclare Offline Compliance System. Your goal is to replace loosely-typed `str` fields in Pydantic schemas with proper `Enum` types for input validation robustness.

### Root Cause Analysis

The review flagged at **Issue #4 (Medium)** that some schema fields accept arbitrary `str` values where they should be constrained to specific enum values, reducing input robustness and maintainability.

### What You Must Do

#### Step 1: Fix `UserRead.role` and `UserRead.status` fields

- **File**: `backend/app/schemas/users.py`, lines 14-15
- **Current**:
  ```python
  role: str
  status: str
  ```
- **Change to**:
  ```python
  role: Role
  status: UserStatus
  ```
- The imports for `Role` and `UserStatus` already exist at line 6: `from app.core.constants import Role, UserStatus`

#### Step 2: Fix `DeclarationRead.state` field

- **File**: `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py`, line 23
- **Current**: `state: str`
- **Change to**: `state: DeclarationState`
- Add import: `from app.core.constants import DeclarationState`

#### Step 3: Fix `PackageVersionRead.state` field

- **File**: `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py`, line 38
- **Current**: `state: str`
- **Change to**: `state: DeclarationState`

#### Step 4: Verify `ProfileRead.profile_status` field

- **File**: `backend/app/schemas/profiles.py`, line 28
- **Current**: `profile_status: str`
- Check if there's a `ProfileStatus` enum in `backend/app/core/constants.py`. If yes, use it. If no, create one with values matching the valid statuses (e.g., `"in_progress"`, `"complete"`).

### Expected Result After Fix

- All API request/response schemas use proper Python enum types for role, status, and state fields
- Invalid enum values in API requests produce `422 Unprocessable Entity` responses with clear validation error messages
- Review criteria **§3.2** (maintainability/extensibility) improves toward **Pass**
- Review criteria **§4.1** (validation) improves toward **Pass**

---

| Issue | Review Sections Affected | Current Verdict | Expected Verdict After Fix |
|-------|-------------------------|-----------------|---------------------------|
| Version timestamp completeness | §1.3.2, §2.1.1, §5.1 | Partially Pass | Pass |
| Database-side encryption | §1.3.2, §2.1.11, §5.1 | Partially Pass | Pass |
| Audit actor attribution | §2.1.8, Issue #3 | Partially Pass | Pass |
| Enum schema tightening | §3.2, §4.1 | Partially Pass | Pass |
| **Overall** | **Final Verdict** | **Partially Pass** | **Pass** |

**Planning tasks and inspections**

I'm in planning mode right now. I need to inspect and outline the plan without making any modifications. It seems I can use tools like read, grep, glob, or task — maybe even todowrite for organizing tasks? Asking targeted questions might be needed if I hit any confusion. Additionally, I should review constants, schemas, and possibly existing tests, especially those for invalid enums. Using read-only methods will help me ensure everything stays intact.

    medical_flags_json: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
    activity_json: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)

def test_nonexistent_ids_return_404(client):
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    reviewer_headers = login_headers(client, "reviewer_demo", "Reviewer#2026")
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    missing = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
    assert client.get(f"/api/v1/declarations/{missing}", headers=participant_headers).status_code == 404
    assert client.get(f"/api/v1/plans/{missing}", headers=participant_headers).status_code == 404
    assert client.post(f"/api/v1/reviews/{missing}/complete", json={"note": "missing"}, headers=reviewer_headers).status_code in {403, 404}
    assert client.post(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{missing}/reset-password", json={"new_password": "ResetPass#2026"}, headers=admin_headers).status_code == 404


def test_schema_validation_and_pagination_edges(client):
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")

content.text:
I checked the relevant schemas and constants. This is a small, safe change, with one important nuance: to get actual `422` request validation for profile status, you should tighten the request schema too, not just `ProfileRead`.

**What I found**
- `backend/app/schemas/users.py:14` still has `UserRead.role: str` and `backend/app/schemas/users.py:15` has `UserRead.status: str`, even though `Role` and `UserStatus` are already imported.
- `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py:25` and `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py:42` still use `str` for declaration/package `state`.
- `backend/app/core/constants.py:15` already defines `DeclarationState`.
- `backend/app/core/constants.py` does not currently define a `ProfileStatus` enum.
- `backend/app/schemas/profiles.py:17` uses `ProfileUpsertRequest.profile_status: str`, and `backend/app/schemas/profiles.py:30` uses `ProfileRead.profile_status: str`.
- Existing enum-validation coverage already lives in `API_tests/test_edge_cases_api.py:62`, but there are no profile/declaration invalid-enum assertions yet.

**Plan**
- Update response schema enums:
  - `backend/app/schemas/users.py`
    - change `UserRead.role` to `Role`
    - change `UserRead.status` to `UserStatus`
  - `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py`
    - import `DeclarationState`
    - change `DeclarationRead.state` to `DeclarationState`
    - change `PackageVersionRead.state` to `DeclarationState`
- Add a new `ProfileStatus` enum:
  - `backend/app/core/constants.py`
  - recommend values:
    - `IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"`
    - `COMPLETE = "complete"`
  - these match current observed usage in seed/tests/services
- Tighten profile schemas with that enum:
  - `backend/app/schemas/profiles.py`
    - import `ProfileStatus`
    - change `ProfileUpsertRequest.profile_status` to `ProfileStatus = ProfileStatus.IN_PROGRESS`
    - change `ProfileRead.profile_status` to `ProfileStatus`
  - I recommend this even though your prompt only explicitly names `ProfileRead`, because otherwise invalid input still won’t 422.
- Add validation coverage in `API_tests/test_edge_cases_api.py`
  - assert invalid profile status on `PUT /api/v1/profiles/me` returns `422`
  - optionally assert invalid declaration state is rejected wherever state is accepted as input, if such request schemas exist; from the files reviewed, declaration state is primarily output-typed here
- Run targeted Docker tests:
  - `API_tests/test_edge_cases_api.py`
  - `API_tests/test_admin_api.py`
  - `API_tests/test_profile_api.py`
  - then optionally full `API_tests`

**Why this is safe**
- These enums are `StrEnum`, so JSON responses should still serialize as the same strings the frontend already expects.
- The biggest behavior change is stricter request validation for profile status, which is the intended improvement.

One recommended scope tweak:
- include `ProfileUpsertRequest.profile_status` in the implementation, not just `ProfileRead`. That’s the part that actually improves input robustness.
Now Continue with the implementation
continue

files.before:
"""404s, validation failures, pagination edges, and empty-state responses."""

from io import BytesIO

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_nonexistent_ids_return_404(client):
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    reviewer_headers = login_headers(client, "reviewer_demo", "Reviewer#2026")
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    missing = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
    assert client.get(f"/api/v1/declarations/{missing}", headers=participant_headers).status_code == 404
    assert client.get(f"/api/v1/plans/{missing}", headers=participant_headers).status_code == 404
    assert client.post(f"/api/v1/reviews/{missing}/complete", json={"note": "missing"}, headers=reviewer_headers).status_code in {403, 404}
    assert client.post(f"/api/v1/admin/users/{missing}/reset-password", json={"new_password": "ResetPass#2026"}, headers=admin_headers).status_code == 404


def test_schema_validation_and_pagination_edges(client):
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")

    bad_register = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "x", "full_name": "x", "password": "short"})
    assert bad_register.status_code == 422

    page_zero = client.get("/api/v1/declarations?page=0&page_size=2", headers=participant_headers)
    assert page_zero.status_code == 422
    negative = client.get("/api/v1/notifications?page=-1&page_size=2", headers=participant_headers)
    assert negative.status_code == 422
    clamped = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users?page=1&page_size=999", headers=admin_headers)
    assert clamped.status_code == 200
    assert clamped.json()["meta"]["page_size"] == 200


def test_empty_state_endpoints(client):
    participant_headers = client.post(
        "/api/v1/auth/register",
        json={"username": "empty_state_user", "full_name": "Empty State User", "password": "EmptyState#2026", "email_optional": "empty@example.local"},
    )
    assert participant_headers.status_code == 200
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "empty_state_user", "EmptyState#2026")
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")

    plans = client.get("/api/v1/plans", headers=participant_headers)
    assert plans.status_code == 200
    assert plans.json()["data"] == []

    declarations = client.get("/api/v1/declarations", headers=participant_headers)
    assert declarations.status_code == 200
    assert declarations.json()["data"] == []

    imports = client.get("/api/v1/imports", headers=admin_headers)
    exports = client.get("/api/v1/exports", headers=admin_headers)
    assert imports.status_code == 200 and imports.json()["data"] == []
    assert exports.status_code == 200 and exports.json()["data"] == []

    empty_audit_page = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=999&page_size=10", headers=admin_headers)
    assert empty_audit_page.status_code == 200
    assert empty_audit_page.json()["data"] == []


def test_invalid_enum_like_admin_and_import_export_inputs_return_422(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(item for item in users if item["username"] == "participant_demo")

    bad_role_create = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/users",
        json={
            "username": "bad_role_user",
            "full_name": "Bad Role",
            "password": "BadRole#2026",
            "role": "superadmin",
        },
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_role_create.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_role_create.text

    bad_status_update = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}",
        json={"status": "deleted"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_status_update.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_status_update.text

    bad_role_update = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}",
        json={"role": "owner"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_role_update.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_role_update.text

    bad_mapping_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/field-mappings",
        json={"name": "Bad format map", "entity_type": "declaration", "format": "pdf", "mapping_json": {"a": "b"}},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_mapping_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_mapping_format.text

    bad_mapping_entity_type = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/field-mappings",
        json={"name": "Bad entity map", "entity_type": "meal", "format": "csv", "mapping_json": {"a": "b"}},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_mapping_entity_type.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_mapping_entity_type.text

    bad_export_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/exports",
        json={"format": "pdf", "scope_type": "declarations"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_export_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_export_format.text

    bad_export_scope = client.post(
        "/api/v1/exports",
        json={"format": "csv", "scope_type": "profiles"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_export_scope.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_export_scope.text

    bad_import_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/imports",
        headers=admin_headers,
        files={"upload": ("bad.csv", BytesIO(b"Package Number\nPKG-1\n"), "text/csv")},
        data={"format": "pdf", "scope_type": "declarations"},
    )
    assert bad_import_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_import_format.text

    negative = client.get("/api/v1/notifications?page=-1&page_size=2", headers=participant_headers)
    assert negative.status_code == 422
    clamped = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users?page=1&page_size=999", headers=admin_headers)
    assert clamped.status_code == 200
    assert clamped.json()["meta"]["page_size"] == 200


def test_empty_state_endpoints(client):
    participant_headers = client.post(
        "/api/v1/auth/register",
        json={"username": "empty_state_user", "full_name": "Empty State User", "password": "EmptyState#2026", "email_optional": "empty@example.local"},
    )
    assert participant_headers.status_code == 200
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "empty_state_user", "EmptyState#2026")
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")

    plans = client.get("/api/v1/plans", headers=participant_headers)
    assert plans.status_code == 200
    assert plans.json()["data"] == []

    declarations = client.get("/api/v1/declarations", headers=participant_headers)
    assert declarations.status_code == 200
    assert declarations.json()["data"] == []

    imports = client.get("/api/v1/imports", headers=admin_headers)
    exports = client.get("/api/v1/exports", headers=admin_headers)
    assert imports.status_code == 200 and imports.json()["data"] == []
    assert exports.status_code == 200 and exports.json()["data"] == []

    empty_audit_page = client.get("/api/v1/audit?page=999&page_size=10", headers=admin_headers)
    assert empty_audit_page.status_code == 200
    assert empty_audit_page.json()["data"] == []


def test_invalid_enum_like_admin_and_import_export_inputs_return_422(client):
    admin_headers = login_headers(client, "admin_demo", "Admin#2026Secure")
    participant_headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    users = client.get("/api/v1/admin/users", headers=admin_headers).json()["data"]
    participant = next(item for item in users if item["username"] == "participant_demo")

    bad_role_create = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/users",
        json={
            "username": "bad_role_user",
            "full_name": "Bad Role",
            "password": "BadRole#2026",
            "role": "superadmin",
        },
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_role_create.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_role_create.text

    bad_status_update = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}",
        json={"status": "deleted"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_status_update.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_status_update.text

    bad_role_update = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/admin/users/{participant['id']}",
        json={"role": "owner"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_role_update.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_role_update.text

    bad_profile_status = client.put(
        "/api/v1/profiles/me",
        json={
            "profile_status": "archived",
            "demographics_json": {},
            "medical_flags_json": {},
            "activity_json": {},
            "anthropometrics_json": {},
            "sensitive": {},
        },
        headers=participant_headers,
    )
    assert bad_profile_status.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_profile_status.text

    bad_mapping_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/field-mappings",
        json={"name": "Bad format map", "entity_type": "declaration", "format": "pdf", "mapping_json": {"a": "b"}},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_mapping_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_mapping_format.text

    bad_mapping_entity_type = client.post(
        "/api/v1/admin/field-mappings",
        json={"name": "Bad entity map", "entity_type": "meal", "format": "csv", "mapping_json": {"a": "b"}},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_mapping_entity_type.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_mapping_entity_type.text

    bad_export_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/exports",
        json={"format": "pdf", "scope_type": "declarations"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_export_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_export_format.text

    bad_export_scope = client.post(
        "/api/v1/exports",
        json={"format": "csv", "scope_type": "profiles"},
        headers=admin_headers,
    )
    assert bad_export_scope.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_export_scope.text

    bad_import_format = client.post(
        "/api/v1/imports",
        headers=admin_headers,
        files={"upload": ("bad.csv", BytesIO(b"Package Number\nPKG-1\n"), "text/csv")},
        data={"format": "pdf", "scope_type": "declarations"},
    )
    assert bad_import_format.status_code == 422
    assert "Input should be" in bad_import_format.text

content.text:
Tightened the enum-like schema fields so role, status, declaration state, and profile status are now properly typed.

- `backend/app/core/constants.py:15` adds `ProfileStatus` with `in_progress` and `complete`.
- `backend/app/schemas/users.py:14` now uses `Role` and `UserStatus` in `UserRead` instead of raw `str`.
- `backend/app/schemas/declarations.py:7` imports `DeclarationState`, and both `DeclarationRead.state` and `PackageVersionRead.state` now use it.
- `backend/app/schemas/profiles.py:6` imports `ProfileStatus`; `ProfileUpsertRequest.profile_status` and `ProfileRead.profile_status` now use the enum, which gives you actual `422` validation on bad input.
- `API_tests/test_edge_cases_api.py:62` now includes a bad profile status request and asserts it fails with `422`.

Verified in Docker:
- `docker compose run --rm -v "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/nutrideclare-offline-compliance-system/repo:/workspace" -w /workspace backend pytest API_tests/test_edge_cases_api.py API_tests/test_admin_api.py API_tests/test_profile_api.py API_tests/test_declaration_lifecycle_api.py`
- Result: `9 passed`

content.text:
Ran the full API suite in Docker again after the enum hardening changes.

- Command: `docker compose run --rm -v "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/nutrideclare-offline-compliance-system/repo:/workspace" -w /workspace backend pytest API_tests`
- Result: `68 passed`

That verifies the full set of fixes together:
- version timestamp serialization
- pgcrypto-only database-side encryption
- admin password reset audit attribution
- enum schema tightening

## Prioritized Issues

### [High] Missing strict schema contract on notification preference update endpoint
- **Impact**: Allows permissive attribute mutation attempts (`payload: dict`) that can cause data integrity issues and weakens compliance-grade API hardening.
- **Evidence**: `backend/app/api/v1/notifications.py:45-48`, `backend/app/services/user_service.py:27-30`.
- **Minimal actionable fix**:
  1. Replace dict payload with typed schema (reuse `NotificationPreferencesUpdate` from `backend/app/schemas/users.py:21-26`).
  2. Whitelist assignable fields explicitly in service layer.
  3. Add API tests for unexpected keys and user_id tampering attempts.

### [Medium] Admin “void declaration” lifecycle action lacks clear frontend operation path
- **Impact**: Full lifecycle control is present in API but not clearly operable in UI flow, weakening completeness claim for role-gated lifecycle operations.
- **Evidence**: `backend/app/api/v1/declarations.py:55-58`, `frontend/src/api/declarations.ts:29-30`, `frontend/src/components/declarations/DeclarationStateActions.vue:3-6`.
- **Minimal actionable fix**:
  1. Add admin declaration detail/list action invoking `declarationsApi.void` with reason input.
  2. Add UI-level role/state gating and API integration tests.

### [Low] Repository hygiene includes generated artifacts/dependencies
- **Impact**: Increased repository noise and potential merge/CI friction.
- **Evidence**: `frontend` directory contains `dist/` and `node_modules/`.
- **Minimal actionable fix**:
  1. Remove generated folders from source control.
  2. Ensure `.gitignore` excludes these paths.

**Implementing user fixes**

It seems the user has prioritized issues they want fixed, so I need to address all of them without needing to ask for more details. I’ll plan to implement these changes in a multi-step process. I’ll definitely need to inspect the relevant files to understand exactly what needs fixing. It feels important to make sure I’m on the right track to meet the user’s needs properly!

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.api.deps import DBSession, get_current_user
from app.core.constants import NotificationType
from app.core.exceptions import AuthorizationError, NotFoundError
from app.core.responses import success_response
from app.repositories.notification_repository import NotificationRepository
from app.services.notification_service import NotificationService
from app.services.user_service import UserService
from app.utils.pagination import PaginationParams, paginate_query

router = APIRouter()


@router.get("")
def list_notifications(db: DBSession, params: PaginationParams = Depends(), user=Depends(get_current_user)):
    repo = NotificationRepository(db)
    paginated = paginate_query(db, repo.stmt_for_user(user.id), params)
    return success_response(paginated.items, meta=paginated.meta)

22:     def update_preferences(self, user_id, payload: dict):
23:         prefs = self.repo.get_preferences(user_id)

36:         validate_password_policy(payload.password)
37:         user = User(

40:             email_optional=payload.email_optional,
41:             role=payload.role,
42:             status=UserStatus.ACTIVE,

48:         self.db.add(NotificationPreference(user_id=user.id))
49:         self.audit.log(actor_user_id=actor.id, action_type="admin_user_create", entity_type="user", entity_id=str(user.id), metadata={"role": user.role})
50:         self.db.commit()
51:         self.db.refresh(user)

from app.core.constants import UserStatus
from app.core.exceptions import NotFoundError
from app.models.user import NotificationPreference, User
from app.repositories.user_repository import UserRepository
from app.security.passwords import hash_password, validate_password_policy
from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
from app.utils.datetime import utc_now


class UserService:
    def __init__(self, db):
        self.db = db
        self.repo = UserRepository(db)
        self.audit = AuditService(db)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.repo.get_by_id(user_id)
        if not user:
            raise NotFoundError("User not found.")
        return user

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def test_notifications_and_mute_settings(client):
    headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    notifications = client.get("/api/v1/notifications", headers=headers)
    assert notifications.status_code == 200
    prefs = client.patch("/api/v1/notifications/preferences", json={"status_changes_enabled": False}, headers=headers)
    assert prefs.status_code == 200
    all_read = client.post("/api/v1/notifications/read-all", headers=headers)
    assert all_read.status_code == 200


def test_notification_mark_read_returns_not_found_for_missing_notification(client):
    headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    response = client.post("/api/v1/notifications/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/read", headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 404

8: 
9: const ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY = 'nutrideclare_access_token'
10: const REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY = 'nutrideclare_refresh_token'
11: const SESSION_USER_KEY = 'nutrideclare_session_user'
12: 
13: export const useAuthStore = defineStore('auth', () => {
14:   const accessToken = ref(localStorage.getItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY) || '')
15:   const refreshToken = ref(localStorage.getItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY) || '')
16:   const user = ref<SessionUser | null>(JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(SESSION_USER_KEY) || 'null'))
17:   const bootstrapping = ref(false)
18: 
19:   function persist() {
20:     localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, accessToken.value)
21:     localStorage.setItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY, refreshToken.value)
22:     localStorage.setItem(SESSION_USER_KEY, JSON.stringify(user.value))
23:     setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
24:   }
25: 
26:   function clearSession() {
27:     accessToken.value = ''
28:     refreshToken.value = ''
29:     user.value = null
30:     persist()
31:   }
32: 
33:   async function hydrate() {
34:     if (!accessToken.value && !refreshToken.value) return
35:     bootstrapping.value = true
36:     setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
37:     try {
38:       user.value = await authApi.me() as SessionUser
39:       persist()
40:     } catch {
41:       try {
42:         if (refreshToken.value) {
43:           const refreshed = await authApi.refresh(refreshToken.value)
44:           accessToken.value = refreshed.access_token
45:           refreshToken.value = refreshed.refresh_token
46:           setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
47:           user.value = await authApi.me() as SessionUser
48:           persist()
49:         }
50:       } catch {
51:         clearSession()
52:       }
53:     } finally {
54:       bootstrapping.value = false
55:     }
56:   }
57: 
58:   async function login(payload: { username: string; password: string; captcha_challenge_token?: string; captcha_answer?: string }) {
59:     const tokenPayload = await authApi.login(payload)
60:     accessToken.value = tokenPayload.access_token
61:     refreshToken.value = tokenPayload.refresh_token
62:     setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
63:     user.value = await authApi.me() as SessionUser
64:     if (tokenPayload.force_password_change) {
65:       user.value.force_password_change = true
66:     }
67:     persist()
68:   }
69: 
70:   async function logout() {
71:     try {
72:       if (refreshToken.value) await authApi.logout(refreshToken.value)
73:     } finally {
74:       clearSession()
75:       await router.replace('/login')
76:     }
77:   }
78: 
79:   async function refreshSession() {
80:     if (!refreshToken.value) {
81:       clearSession()
82:       await router.replace('/login')
83:       return
84:     }
85:     try {
86:       const tokenPayload = await authApi.refresh(refreshToken.value)
87:       accessToken.value = tokenPayload.access_token
88:       refreshToken.value = tokenPayload.refresh_token
89:       setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
90:       user.value = await authApi.me() as SessionUser
91:       persist()
92:     } catch {
93:       clearSession()
94:       await router.replace('/login')
95:     }
96:   }
97: 
98:   async function changeForcedPassword(new_password: string) {
99:     await authApi.completeForcedPasswordChange({ new_password })
100:     if (user.value) {
101:       user.value.force_password_change = false
102:       persist()
103:     }
104:   }
105: 
106:   const isAuthenticated = computed(() => Boolean(accessToken.value && user.value))
107:   const role = computed<UserRole | null>(() => user.value?.role ?? null)
108: 
109:   setUnauthorizedHandler(async () => {
110:     if (refreshToken.value) {
111:       try {
112:         await refreshSession()
113:       } catch {
114:         clearSession()
115:       }
116:     }
117:   })
118:   setAccessToken(accessToken.value)
119:

127:     hydrate,
128:     login,

133:   }
134: })

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.api.deps import DBSession, get_current_user, require_roles
from app.core.constants import DeclarationState, Role
from app.core.responses import success_response
from app.security.permissions import ensure_package_access, ensure_package_owner
from app.schemas.declarations import CorrectionAcknowledgeRequest, CorrectionResubmitRequest, DeclarationCreateRequest, PackageVersionRead, StateChangeRequest
from app.services.declaration_service import DeclarationService
from app.utils.pagination import PaginationParams, paginate_query

router = APIRouter()


@router.get("")
def list_declarations(db: DBSession, params: PaginationParams = Depends(), user=Depends(get_current_user)):
    service = DeclarationService(db)
    paginated = paginate_query(db, service.repo.stmt_for_user(user), params)
    return success_response(paginated.items, meta=paginated.meta)

from datetime import datetime

from API_tests.conftest import login_headers


def _assert_iso_8601(value: str):
    assert isinstance(value, str)
    assert value
    datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace("Z", "+00:00"))


def test_declaration_lifecycle_endpoints(client):
    headers = login_headers(client, "participant_demo", "Participant#2026")
    profile = client.get("/api/v1/profiles/me", headers=headers).json()["data"]
    plan = client.get("/api/v1/plans", headers=headers).json()["data"][0]
    created = client.post("/api/v1/declarations", json={"profile_id": profile["id"], "plan_id": plan["id"]}, headers=headers)
    package_id = created.json()["data"]["id"]
    submitted = client.post(f"/api/v1/declarations/{package_id}/submit", json={}, headers=headers)
    assert submitted.status_code == 200
    withdrawn = client.post(f"/api/v1/declarations/{package_id}/withdraw", json={}, headers=headers)
